hero.question: Counter-Strike: B8 vs Wildcard (BO3) - PGL Bucharest Group Stage
hero.prob_take: B8 is priced at 91% to win, which means the market has essentially handed them the match, leaving almost no room for error or upset. Wildcard would need B8 to have a catastrophic collapse or face a team dramatically better than expected, but the Counter-Strike fundamentals (map pool, firepower, recent form) don't support a 9% underdog scenario here.

daily_take.headline: B8 at 91% is a trap. The sharp money isn't convinced.
daily_take.deck: B8 jumped 16.5 points in 24 hours on what looks like line movement from sharp money rotating positions. But 91% odds on a BO3 in esports is aggressive for a team without recent LAN dominance. The market is pricing in near certainty, which rarely exists in best-of-threes. Wildcard has shown up in meaningful moments before. The play is either fading the consensus at even money or waiting for B8 to hit 95% before considering a lay bet.
